$comment: Rubric for banker-grade slide quality with controlled modern freshness. Base grammar is investment-bank / strategy-consulting: action title, one claim, aligned evidence, source discipline, consistent title/source placement, and a clear grid/flex contract. Freshness is rewarded only when it improves composition, hierarchy, density, and impact without weakening auditability. Defect-deduction scoring: each category starts at full points; deduct for concrete observed defects (minor=-1, major=-3, critical=-5 within the category, floor 0). Total = max 100. Judge of record is an independent reviewer in the other CLI's non-interactive mode, run on rendered PNGs via eval_deck.py (Claude Code host -> --judge codex; Codex host -> --judge claude), iterating to >=95. Every reported defect must be re-verified against the actual render before fixing.

categories.name: Story Structure And Message
criteria: Content-slide titles are action titles that state conclusions, and the title read-through forms one coherent argument. Cover, agenda, section dividers, and quote-only close slides are structural exceptions.
The same slide's chart, table, value, diagram, or evidence directly proves the title.
Each slide has one message and one protagonist; no competing focal object or second claim.
Numbers are consistent across title, body, chart, table, source, and other slides.
The deck covers the decision-critical issues for the audience without generic completeness or self-introduction pages.

categories.name: Layout And Density
criteria: No overflow, clipping, overlap, or broken render artifacts.
Whitespace is balanced: no accidental large empty zones and no cramped clusters.
The investment-bank / strategy-consulting base is visible: stable header, clear evidence field, comparable rows/columns, and disciplined source area.
Modern freshness comes from scale contrast, asymmetry, proof rails, dense but readable fields, and composition rather than decorative effects.
The grid/flex contract is visible in the render: role map, column spans, alignment spine, body bands, edge locks, and consistent gaps.
Major body blocks are generally three or fewer unless the primitive explicitly supports structured multiples.

categories.name: Typography
criteria: Hierarchy is clear: title > heading > body/proof > metric subline > source.
Type size and weight are consistent across comparable slides.
Important body evidence is readable from the back row; only footer/source text may be small.
Japanese typesetting is clean: no awkward wrapping, full-width alphanumeric drift, or line-initial punctuation.
YoY, delta, vs plan, and prior-year text sit on a separate metric subline with visible air from the value line.

categories.name: Color And Design Discipline
criteria: Act palette usage is disciplined: deep green/teal base, controlled tints, gray support, and no uncontrolled color spread.
Yellow accent appears no more than once per slide and only for a true protagonist mark.
Time-series emphasis is consistent: latest/conclusion period is strongest, prior periods recede.
Color meaning remains stable across the deck.
No gradients, shadows, neon, pure black, or decorative effects are used to manufacture impact.

categories.name: Chart And Evidence Craft
criteria: Charts are minimal, direct-labeled when practical, and use axes only when needed.
Bar/column charts provide comparison across periods, scenarios, segments, plans, peers, or prior year; no lone current-value bar.
Units, signs, decimals, denominators, and labels are consistent.
The one point supporting the conclusion is visible within three seconds.
Actuals, forecasts, plans, targets, and assumptions are visually distinguishable.
Tables use light rules, readable row heights, and a highlighted row/column that corresponds to the title.

categories.name: Source And Footnote Discipline
criteria: Evidence slides include real-looking sources; no placeholders or impossible citations.
Internal assumptions are separated from external sources.
No material number appears without provenance, period, unit, or denominator.
Source, assumption, and note remain small and restrained without interfering with the main proof.
Future-looking claims include assumption or caveat language rather than promise-like certainty.
